requirements.description:
AsyncManager does not override the BaseManager.connect() method, so the
      inherited implementation is a plain synchronous function. All async test
      helpers call connect() via _run(), which wraps the return value with
      asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(). A non-coroutine return
      value causes: TypeError: An asyncio.Future, a coroutine or an awaitable
      is required. This single missing async override is the root cause of
      approximately 42 failing tests across test_manager.py, test_server.py,
      and test_pubsub_manager.py in the async test suite.
requirements.contract:
AsyncManager must provide an async override of connect() with the same
      signature as BaseManager.connect(eio_sid, namespace). The method must be
      declared with async def so that it returns a coroutine object when
      called. Its observable behavior must be identical to the base class:
      generate a new socket ID, register the client in the namespace room
      structure, and return the new sid (or None if a ValueDuplicationError
      occurs). All side effects (bidict insertion, room initialization) must
      remain the same as in BaseManager.connect().

      Signature:
        async def connect(self, eio_sid, namespace) -> str | None
requirements.acceptance_tests:
All tests in tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager that call
      _run(self.bm.connect(...)) must pass, including but not limited to:
        tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager::test_connect
        tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager::test_disconnect
        tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager::test_emit_to_all
        tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager::test_rooms
        tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager::test_get_namespaces
        tests/async/test_manager.py::TestAsyncManager::test_get_participants
      And all other async manager, server, and pubsub manager tests that fail
      with "TypeError: An asyncio.Future, a coroutine or an awaitable is
      required" due to a non-awaitable return from connect().

requirements.description:
SimpleClient.connect() does not accept a wait_timeout keyword argument.
      When tests call client.connect(..., wait_timeout='w'), Python raises:
      TypeError: SimpleClient.connect() got an unexpected keyword argument
      'wait_timeout'. Furthermore, tests assert that wait_timeout is forwarded
      verbatim to the underlying self.client.connect() call. The current
      implementation omits this parameter entirely from the signature and from
      the forwarded call.
requirements.contract:
SimpleClient.connect() must accept an additional optional keyword
      argument wait_timeout (with a default of None or any suitable sentinel)
      and must pass it through to the underlying Client.connect() call.

      Updated signature (minimum change):
        def connect(self, url, headers={}, auth=None, transports=None,
                    namespace='/', socketio_path='socket.io',
                    wait_timeout=None)

      The value of wait_timeout must be passed as a keyword argument named
      wait_timeout in the self.client.connect(...) invocation, so that the
      underlying transport layer receives it unchanged.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/common/test_simple_client.py::TestSimpleClient::test_connect
      tests/common/test_simple_client.py::TestSimpleClient::test_connect_context_manager

      Both tests must pass. Each asserts that mock_client().connect was called
      with wait_timeout='w'.

requirements.description:
AsyncSimpleClient.connect() has the same missing wait_timeout parameter
      problem as SimpleClient.connect(). Tests call
      client.connect(..., wait_timeout='w') and expect the value to be
      forwarded to the underlying async client's connect() call.
      Python raises: TypeError: AsyncSimpleClient.connect() got an unexpected
      keyword argument 'wait_timeout'.
requirements.contract:
AsyncSimpleClient.connect() must accept wait_timeout as an optional
      keyword argument and forward it to the awaited self.client.connect()
      call.

      Updated signature (minimum change):
        async def connect(self, url, headers={}, auth=None, transports=None,
                          namespace='/', socketio_path='socket.io',
                          wait_timeout=None)

      The value of wait_timeout must appear as a keyword argument named
      wait_timeout in the await self.client.connect(...) call.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/async/test_simple_client.py::TestAsyncAsyncSimpleClient::test_connect
      tests/async/test_simple_client.py::TestAsyncAsyncSimpleClient::test_connect_context_manager

      Both tests must pass. Each asserts that mock_client().connect.mock was
      called with wait_timeout='w'.

requirements.location:
code/src/socketio/simple_client.py — class SimpleClient, property sid()
      code/src/socketio/async_simple_client.py — class AsyncSimpleClient,
      property sid()
requirements.description:
Both SimpleClient.sid and AsyncSimpleClient.sid currently return
      self.client.sid (attribute access). The tests configure the mock client
      as follows:
        client.client = mock.MagicMock(transport='websocket')
        client.client.get_sid.return_value = 'sid'
      and then assert:
        assert client.sid == 'sid'
      Because the property accesses the .sid attribute of the mock (which
      returns another MagicMock, not 'sid'), the assertion fails. The
      underlying client object exposes a get_sid() method rather than a .sid
      attribute for obtaining the current session ID.
requirements.contract:
The sid property in both SimpleClient and AsyncSimpleClient must be
      changed to call self.client.get_sid() instead of accessing
      self.client.sid. The property must still return None when self.client is
      None (i.e., when not connected).

      Expected behavior:
        @property
        def sid(self):
            return self.client.get_sid() if self.client else None

      This change applies identically to both SimpleClient (sync) and
      AsyncSimpleClient (async).
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/common/test_simple_client.py::TestSimpleClient::test_properties
      tests/async/test_simple_client.py::TestAsyncAsyncSimpleClient::test_properties

      Both tests must pass, verifying that client.sid returns the value
      returned by client.client.get_sid().
